original_text: Home.
Home healthcare. See Home Care Indications for Admission Section HC in Heart Failure guideline in Home Care.
Recovery facility care. See Recovery Facility Care Indications for Admission Section RFC in Heart Failure guideline in Recovery Facility Care.

Inpatient rehabilitation facility. See Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (Acute Rehabilitation) Indications for Admission Section RFC in Inpatient Rehabilitation Facility (Acute Rehabilitation): Cardiac Disorders guideline in Recovery Facility Care.
Assessment of needs and planning for care, including ( 46 ) ( 47 ):
Nutrition and hydration management ( 52 ) ( 53 )
Ongoing education required ( 53 )
Psychosocial assessment, management, and referrals ( 54 )
Early identification of anticipated discharge destination; options include ( 47 ) ( 55 ):
Home; considerations include:
Home safety assessment. See Home Safety Assessment SR for further information.
Patient safe to go home; examples include ( 56 ) ( 57 ) ( 58 ):
Medical status stable for patient's condition
Functional care can safely be provided with available resources.
Mental status stable for patient's condition
Develop and modify treatment plan (involving multiple providers) as needed.
Medication availability confirmed and reconciliation complete
Patient/caregiver education completed with written discharge instructions provided
Community resources identified and referrals made, as needed
Home care arranged, if indicated
Necessary medical equipment delivery arranged or available in home, if indicated
Necessary medical supplies ordered, or patient/caregiver can obtain, if indicated
Access to follow-up care
Self-management ability if appropriate. See Activities of Daily Living (ADL) and Instrumental Activities of Daily Living (IADL) Assessment SR for further information.
Caregiver need, ability, and availability
Post-acute skilled care or custodial care as indicated. See Discharge Planning Tool SR for further information.
Evaluate and address preadmission functioning as needed.
Transitions of care plan complete, including ( 47 ) ( 55 ) ( 59 ):
Patient and caregiver education complete.
See Teach-Back Tool SR for further information.
See Heart Failure: Patient Education for Clinicians SR for further information.
Medication reconciliation completion includes ( 51 ) ( 60 ):
Compare patient's discharge list of medications (prescribed and over-the-counter) against provider's admission or transfer orders.
Assess each medication for correlation to disease state or medical condition.
Report medication discrepancies to prescribing provider, attending physician, and primary care provider, and ensure accurate medication order is identified.
Provide reconciled medication list to all treating providers.
Confirm that patient or caregiver can acquire medication.
Evaluate and address psychosocial status issues as indicated. See Psychosocial Assessment SR for further information.
Educate patient and caregiver.
Provide complete medication list to patient and caregiver.
Importance of presenting personal medication list to all providers at each care transition, including all provider appointments
Reason, dosage, and timing of medication (eg, use "teach-back" techniques) ( 61 )
Encourage communication between patient, caregiver, and pharmacy for obtaining prescriptions, setting up home medication delivery, and reviewing for drug-drug interactions.
See Medication Reconciliation Tool SR for further information.
Plan communicated to patient, caregiver, and all members of care team, including ( 62 ):
Inpatient care and service providers
Primary care provider
All post-discharge care and service providers
Evaluate and address social determinants of health (eg, housing, food). See Social Determinants of Health Screening Tool SR for further information. ( 48 ) ( 49 )
Appointments planned or scheduled, which may include:
Primary care provider ( 52 ) ( 63 ) ( 64 )
Cardiologist ( 65 )
Dietitian ( 52 )
Follow-up evaluation call. See Post-Acute Care Follow-Up Call Tool SR for further information. ( 1 ) ( 2 ) ( 63 ) ( 66 )
Palliative or end-of-life care provider ( 67 )
Rehabilitation therapy services ( 2 ) ( 52 )
Specialists for management of comorbidities as needed ( 68 )
Other
Outpatient testing and procedure plans made, which may include:
Evaluate and address patient or caregiver preferences as indicated.
Echocardiogram
Exercise stress test ( 52 )
Laboratory testing ( 69 )
Other
Referrals made for assistance or support, which may include:
Alcohol and other drug abuse or dependence treatment ( 70 )
Community services
Educational program (eg, chronic condition management, self-management) ( 64 ) ( 69 ) ( 71 )
End-stage disease planning ( 52 )
Financial, for follow-up care, medication, and transportation
Identify skilled services needed at next level of care, with specific attention to:
Self-help or support groups ( 52 )
Social services (eg, social programs, advance directives) ( 71 )
Tobacco use treatment ( 52 ) ( 69 ) ( 70 ) ( 72 )
Other
Medical equipment and supplies coordinated (ie, delivered or delivery confirmed), which may include ( 52 ):
Blood pressure cuff
Weight scale ( 2 )
Other
Cardiovascular status assessment ( 50 )
Medication management, adherence instruction, and side effects assessment ( 51 )
New need for oxygen therapy to keep oxygen saturation above 90% (or increased FiO 2 need from baseline)
Has not improved sufficiently with observation care (eg, appropriately dosed IV diuretics) [ B ]
Post-hospital levels of admission may include:
Discharge planning includes [ M ] :
Hemodynamic instability ( 1 ) ( 2 ) ( 3 ) ( 7 ) ( 8 ) ( 9 )
Altered mental status that is severe or persistent ( 8 )
Increased creatinine with reduction of more than 50% in estimated glomerular filtration rate from baseline [ C ] eGFR - Adult Calculator ( 3 )
Progressively (ongoing) rising creatinine with reduction of more than 25% in estimated glomerular filtration rate from baseline [ C ] eGFR - Adult Calculator ( 3 ) ( 7 )
Pulmonary artery catheter monitoring needed [ D ] ( 1 ) ( 8 )
Severe electrolyte abnormalities requiring inpatient care ( 3 ) ( 7 )
Cardiac arrhythmias of immediate concern ( 3 ) ( 7 )
Acute myocardial ischemia causing or associated with failure. See Angina ISC or Myocardial Infarction ISC guideline as appropriate. ( 1 ) ( 3 ) ( 7 )
Pulmonary edema that is very severe (eg, invasive or noninvasive assisted ventilation needed, [ A ] imminent, or likely; or need for 100% oxygen to keep oxygen saturation above 90%) ( 3 ) ( 10 )
Anasarca or peripheral edema that is severe (eg, significant paroxysmal dyspnea, inability to ambulate or void, skin breakdown, concomitant ascites) ( 2 ) ( 11 )
Pulmonary edema and ALL of the following ( 3 ) ( 7 ):
Tachypnea that persists despite observation care ( 7 )
Dyspnea (above baseline) that persists despite observation care ( 7 )
